name: Eta HTTP/1.1 server tail latency over real sockets

description: Baseline: Eta H1 server tail latency over real sockets (oha HTTP/1.1, 16 keep-alive conns, n=40000, median of 3). p99 geomean 221µs; static_1k highest (300µs).

asi.hypothesis: baseline of Eta H1 server tail latency over real sockets (oha HTTP/1.1, 16 keep-alive connections, n=40000, median of 3)
asi.next: Apply the proven H2 playbook. Biggest expected lever: per-request request-head-read Fiber.first + with_timeout in h1_server_connection.ml (~1054, ~1093) -- mirror the H2 handler-switch + per-request-fiber removal. Then handler/write/body timeouts -> per-connection watchdog (H2 #4/#5), and gate request_metrics on metrics_enabled (H2 #8).
asi.regime_note: H1 absolute latencies are ~10x lower than H2 (p99 geomean 221us vs H2 2540us) because H1 has no stream multiplexing contention -- each of the 16 connections handles one request at a time. rps_geomean 85k (lower than H2 164k since no multiplexing). Noise floor will be smaller in absolute us but likely similar in relative terms.
asi.tail_profile: p99 geomean 221us. static_1k highest p99 (300us, body-bearing). root lowest (166us). p99/p50 ratios: root 1.39x, user_id 1.31x, static 1.29x, echo 1.31x -- much tighter than H2 (2-3.3x), so H1 tail is already less spiky; gains likely come more from reducing per-request fixed overhead (forks, allocs) than from GC pause reduction.

description: H1 run_handler: replace per-request Fiber.first + with_timeout with per-connection watchdog + Cancel.sub (handler_watch slot). p99 geomean -9.2%, p50 -12.8%, rps +11.9%

asi.hypothesis: H1 run_handler did a per-request Eio.Fiber.first (fork fiber + cancel ctx + promise to race closed_signal) AND a per-request Eio.Time.with_timeout (sleeper fiber + Zzz node) for handler_timeout. Replace both with the proven H2 watchdog pattern: per-connection watchdog daemon + a single Eio.Cancel.sub per request registered in t.handler_watch; mark_closed cancels it on close, watchdog cancels on deadline. Distinguish close vs timeout by t.closed.
asi.next: Continue the H1 playbook: (1) read_request_head_with_timeout still arms a per-request with_timeout (~1093) -- replace with watchdog/deadline; (2) gate request_metrics + connection_metrics on Eta.Runtime.metrics_enabled (~1018, ~1212); (3) per-response micro-opts. Re-profile to confirm the new hot path.
asi.result: p99 geomean 220.77->200.47 (-9.2%, clears noise floor). All endpoints improved: root p99 166->139, user_id 234->206, static 300->280, echo 204->201. Low-noise confirmations: p50_geomean 166->145 (-12.8%), rps_geomean 85229->95387 (+11.9%). RSS flat 31MB.
asi.validation: all H1+shared unit suites pass (checks 3.5s). Semantics preserved: handler_timeout still enforced via watchdog (deadline cancel -> Eio.Time.Timeout -> handler_timeout_error); connection close still aborts in-flight handler (mark_closed cancels the cancel ctx; t.closed distinguishes -> connection_closed_error). Both still force_close=true as before. The per-request fiber fork + sleeper fork are gone.
